query: I’m looking to model a complete, end-to-end digital-twin system for real-time monitoring of electric-vehicle battery degradation and capacity loss: at the top level, it should represent the high-voltage battery pack identified by serial number bp-20231101-07, manufactured by VoltEdge (a specialist in high-power energy storage), with a nominal capacity of 212.5 ampere-hours and a nominal cell-stack voltage of 400.0 volts, meeting typical medium-voltage system requirements and the electrical specifications for modular power architectures so its physical and electrical characteristics can be used for predictable performance modeling; within that pack, it should include a high-performance module mod-0047 built from 96 nickel-manganese-cobalt (NMC) cells, each rated to a maximum voltage of 4.2 V, with all parameters statically configured in the module’s property set to meet stringent automotive and grid-storage specifications; it should also incorporate localized thermal monitoring via the temperature sensor temp-sensor-01 installed in battery-pack-1-module-3, where heat most critically impacts performance and safety, with the sensor last calibrated on 2024-09-15 and meeting the required tolerance limits for the pack’s operating temperature range, and maintenance schedules referencing that calibration date to determine the next verification to ensure traceability and compliance throughout the energy-storage system’s lifecycle; finally, it should track the predictive analytics configuration as immutable properties so the logic is traceable and up-to-date, specifically that it runs model version 1.3.0 with the latest schema revisions, uses a randomForest algorithm as its core inference engine to balance bias and variance, and had its parameters and feature-importance metrics last refreshed on 2024-09-30 to provide engineers a clear baseline for data-drift monitoring and re-training cycles suitable for integration into larger pipelines.
